#### What's great

I love Craft.do and used it for my second brain with the PARA system, see screenshot (sorry it's in Spanish):

I love it as it has some of the options that Notion offers (but without the learning curve) and it's more visually than Google Docs.   
  
Plus I can customize it with colours, and many things.   
  
4.5 🌟 100% recommended.

#### What needs improvement [...] #### vs Alternatives

Notion
Google Docs

A friend recommended the tool. He showed me how he built his second brain on Craft.do. I was soooo in love with the visual options and easy to use platform, that I fell in love with the tool. Very recommended, as it's easier than Notion (I also used this for many things, and will review it shortly) and more customizable than Google Docs.   
  
I use it as well for Client's documents & procedures and it's great. Better than the other 2 options mentioned.

# Do you write reviews after your trips?

by•

Why or why not?

3 views

### Replies

Best

Matthew Grant

Only if the experience is bad or truly over-the-top great.

Report

Sanja Micic

@matthew\_grant That sounds fair! :)

Report

Aleksandar Blazhev

ZeroHuman.

ZeroHuman.

Depends. Sometimes I love to share good experiences. Sometimes to warn others about possible problems. Sometimes don't have the energy to write a review.

Report

Sanja Micic

@byalexai Every contribution you make is valuable and appreciated by the travel community! :)

Report

Marie-Philippe Leblanc

Fleso

Fleso [...] Report

Marie-Philippe Leblanc

Fleso

Fleso

I do! But mostly when I've had bad experiences. To warn others I guess?

Report

Sanja Micic

@mplebl That makes a lot of sense. It helps travelers make informed decisions and avoid potential pitfalls.

Report

Gurkaran Singh

I usually write reviews after my trips, it's like leaving breadcrumbs for other travelers to follow! Plus, it's my way of giving back to the travel community.

Report

Sanja Micic

@thestarkster That's wonderful! ✈️✨

results.title: Pulldog: A Mac application to keep your code reviews organized! | Product Hunt
results.content:
Pulldog doesn't pretend to replace Github or Gitlab; it’s here to fit alongside them — but in a way that makes macOS feel like the best place to do your reviews 90% of the time.

I’d love to get your feedback & suggestions — whether you’re a dev lead, reviewer, or contributor. If something feels off or missing, please let me know. It’s still evolving every week, and your input can really shape where it goes next.

Report

Elvis Bueno [...] (1) Git account/repositories explosions

As a Swift developer I often had to do code review on multiple repositories, the ones for my team app, the ones for libraries that gravitates over app(s). Those numbers increase if mono-repository are not part of the equation. I also contribute to some open source projects sometimes so I need to keep an eye on those as well. And I was a bit frustrated that in 2025, I had to either monitor my email(s) or jump from one Github account to another. Because in a perfect world you have one git account to contribute to all of this but in practice you may have a personal account, and a profesional account. You can even work sometimes on Github for perso and on Gitlab for work. [...] 🔔 Notifications: only subscribe to specific channels and to specific repositories and receive system notifications. No more email(s).
 🧠 Review with Apple Intelligence (macOS 26+): On-device AI at no extra-cost that summarizes files and evaluates PRs locally — no code ever leaves your machine.
 🔍 Spotlight Actions: run PR actions right from Spotlight like “merge my mergeables” or “rerun failed pipelines.”
 🧩 Widgets: track reviews and team progress right from your Desktop or Notification Center.
 🪄 Auto-commit filtering: instantly see what changed since your last review or approval.
 🎨 Themes: 90+ themes & 185 languages supported.
 🎭 Memojify mode: replace missing avatars with Memojis to make reviews a little more human.

results.title: ClickSay Reviews - What's Your Honest Take?
results.content:
@fredmaker Heyy sorry for not messaging sooner. The thing is what exactly does your product do, because I don't think I absolutely understood like what it is about yet. And there might be too much writing. A little less writing, and more punctual, effective summarization does much better and I think can get the message to a more general audience. Not just a certain group. I just would say in 1 or two sentences what your product does EXACTLY. And lessen the writing but make it more effective to reach all people, the interested, the uninterested, the educated and uneducated with hooks. [...] Sorry if this is not making sense or not good enough. If I was me, I would make it for all kinds of people, and make a clear effective sentence telling EXACTLY what my product would do. That's all. Otherwise everything looks really really nice!! [...] If you haven't left a review yet, it takes like 2 minutes and genuinely makes a difference for other makers finding the extension. Plus it helps us understand if we're solving the real problems people have.

Check it out here if you want to add your thoughts - 

What's been the most useful part of ClickSay for your workflow so far, or what would actually make you use it more?

54 views

### Replies

Best

Dan

results.title: How do I connect review notifications to Slack? | Product Hunt Help Center
results.content:
Product Hunt Help Center

# How do I connect review notifications to Slack?

Written by Jake Crump

Keeping track of new reviews helps you keep a pulse on what your customers are saying about your product and gives you the opportunity to respond and build trust with existing or new users.

To avoid spamming you, we bundle email notifications that alert you about new reviews.

If you're interested in getting a time-sensitive notification each time a new review comes in, we suggest adding this integration to your team's Slack.

[](

## Steps to set up:

1. Search for your Product Page

   [](
2. If you haven't claimed your Product Page, do so
3. Click "Edit page"

   [](
4. Go to the "Notifications" tab and turn the toggle on
5. Choose the Slack channel you'd like notifications to be sent to
